@@ -445,7 +445,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.5.1","title":"[FNX] Ordering subtask: centrality cache and stable normalization","description":"Goal
Implement centrality cache and deterministic consumption in layout pipeline.

Scope
- Cache keyed by graph hash and projection mode.
- Inject scores into node priority map without unstable floating ordering.
- Add deterministic quantization/normalization policy.

Acceptance Criteria
- Cache hit/miss behavior is traceable.
- No nondeterministic ordering in repeated runs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.717538332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T18:43:08.876022562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T18:43:08.875816673Z","close_reason":"Implemented fnx_ordering.rs module with: QuantizedScore for deterministic ordering, compute_centrality_scores for caching, compare_with_centrality for tie-breaking, centrality_cache_key for stable cache keys. 11 unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","fnx","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.717538332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.5.2","title":"[FNX] Ordering subtask: ablation benchmark and adoption threshold","description":"Goal
Create an ablation benchmark to validate quality gain versus runtime cost for centrality-assisted ordering.

Scope
- Run baseline and fnx-assisted variants over representative corpus.
- Measure crossings, edge length variance, rank compactness, total layout ms.
- Establish adoption thresholds.

Acceptance Criteria
- Decision report clearly states when fnx centrality should be enabled by default.
- CI has deterministic benchmark-mode smoke checks.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.795128125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T18:45:46.453303293Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T18:45:46.452847661Z","close_reason":"Added ablation benchmark with AblationResult/AblationSummary structs, run_ablation_single function, and 5 CI-friendly tests. Thresholds: centrality recommended when < 1ms (1000us). Tests verify small graph fast, medium graph reasonable, determinism, and adoption smoke check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","fnx","layout-quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.795128125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.6","title":"[FNX] Witness and telemetry integration across parser/layout/CLI/WASM","description":"Goal
Plumb fnx complexity witnesses into frankenmermaid traces so algorithm decisions are explainable and auditable.

Scope
- Map fnx witness fields into LayoutTrace and parser diagnostics metadata.
- Add CLI JSON emission for witness artifacts.
- Ensure wasm APIs can surface witness/analysis metadata for browser tooling.

Acceptance Criteria
- Witness data is present, deterministic, and schema-documented.
- Traces clearly connect decision -> algorithm -> witness -> output quality.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.873789065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T19:09:23.469251303Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T19:09:23.468587025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fnx","telemetry","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:36.873789065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.862940767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:11.805328618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.931053632Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.997221123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:11.746579679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.7","title":"[FNX] Directed capability gap-closure plan with franken_networkx upstream","description":"Goal
Close directed-algorithm gaps in franken_networkx needed for deeper layout integration in later phases.

Background
Current Rust fnx capabilities are strongest on undirected Graph operations. Deeper replacement of directed layout internals requires DiGraph-native algorithms and semantics.

Scope
- Define required directed primitives for frankenmermaid layout integration.
- Coordinate upstream implementation plan and versioning.
- Add compatibility tests in frankenmermaid against targeted fnx versions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Directed capability matrix exists with explicit status per required algorithm.
- Version pinning and upgrade policy documented.
- Integration tests fail fast on unsupported fnx capability regressions.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests

- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

6) Quality gates and build policy

- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.094065988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:23:25.175584022Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["digraph","fnx","upstream"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:37.094065988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.7","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:52:38.063891746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.7.1","title":"[FNX] Upstream subtask: minimum directed algorithm surface","description":"Goal
Specify and implement the minimum directed algorithm set needed for phase-2 layout integration.

Scope
- Candidate set: SCC, weakly/strongly connected components, directed cycle basis alternatives, directed bridges/cut-like signals where appropriate.
- Define deterministic output ordering contracts.
- Add conformance fixtures mirroring frankenmermaid integration scenarios.

Acceptance Criteria
- Directed algorithms exist upstream with documented complexity and determinism behavior.
- frankenmermaid test corpus exercises these APIs through adapter layer.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.8.1","title":"[FNX] Rollout gate: Phase-1 enablement (undirected intelligence + diagnostics)","description":"Goal
Enable production-ready Phase-1 rollout (undirected structural intelligence + diagnostics + UX controls) with strict determinism and regression guardrails.

Scope
- Gate launch of fnx-assisted undirected diagnostics, tie-break enrichment, telemetry, and user controls.
- Require compatibility baseline + differential reporting + migration safeguards before widening exposure.
- Validate rollback signals and canary policy for Phase-1 surfaces.

User Impact
- Delivers immediate value sooner (diagnostics clarity, safer defaults, actionable controls) without waiting on directed algorithm upstream work.
- Preserves trust with deterministic evidence and rollback readiness.

Acceptance Criteria
- Phase-1 go/no-go decision is evidence-backed and reproducible.
- Canary and rollback checks pass for Phase-1 feature set.
- Default policy for Phase-1 modes is explicitly documented.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","title":"[FNX] Rollout gate: Phase-2 directed capability enablement","description":"Goal
Gate Phase-2 rollout for directed-layout semantics and upstream fnx directed capability adoption.

Scope
- Validate directed algorithm readiness, determinism, and version compatibility.
- Run directed-specific quality/performance/parity scenarios and regression gates.
- Decide enablement policy for any directed fnx-assisted behaviors.

User Impact
- Prevents exposing immature directed behavior while preserving a clear path to deeper future improvements.
- Ensures phase-2 capabilities are only shipped with strong evidence and rollback confidence.

Acceptance Criteria
- Directed capability matrix and version policy are validated against actual integration results.
- Directed scenario suite passes determinism, quality, and performance gates.
- Directed rollout policy and rollback triggers are explicitly documented.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:36:07.692953176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:37.655301027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["digraph","fnx","phase2","qa","rollout","upstream"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:37.655249892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.717320294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.915569748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:37.263720362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.12.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:37.398660758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.240805693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.376704885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.575818698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:38:36.025329781Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9","title":"[FNX] User-facing controls and semantic enhancement surfaces","description":"Goal
Turn fnx integration into directly visible user value, not just internal algorithm improvements.

Scope
- Expose user-facing controls for fnx analysis behavior in config and CLI.
- Add semantic enhancement options driven by fnx analysis (centrality/critical-path emphasis, structural insights).
- Add clear UX copy for why a diagnostic/recommendation is emitted and what the user can do.
- Provide practical examples demonstrating when to enable/disable fnx modes.

User Value
- Faster debugging of large/complex diagrams.
- Better readability through optional semantic emphasis.
- Transparent and controllable behavior instead of hidden heuristics.

Acceptance Criteria
- New user-facing knobs are documented and discoverable.
- Enhancements are opt-in/feature-gated with deterministic defaults.
- Example corpus shows measurable readability/diagnostic clarity improvement.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.144592108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:31:57.085481753Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","diagnostics","fnx","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.144592108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.113932379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.171492881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.229329159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.2","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: optional centrality/critical-path semantic styling","description":"Goal
Implement optional semantic auto-styling powered by fnx metrics for high-information diagrams.

Scope
- Centrality-driven node emphasis (heatmap/intensity classes).
- Optional critical-path edge emphasis for flow readability.
- Style output compatible with SVG, terminal where feasible, and wasm APIs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Styling is deterministic for identical input/config.
- Default output unchanged unless feature is enabled.
- Visual regression tests verify expected style classes and element counts.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: structural recommendations and remediation hints","description":"Goal
Add structural recommendation UX that translates fnx findings into concrete user guidance.

Scope
- Suggest simplifications (bridge hotspots, articulation choke points, disconnected islands).
- Provide recommendation categories with confidence and rationale.
- Support CLI + wasm payloads for front-end display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Recommendations are stable and machine-readable.
- Each recommendation includes rationale + suggested remediation.
- E2E scenarios validate recommendations for representative anti-pattern graphs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.4","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: user guide, examples, and troubleshooting","description":"Goal
Ship a user-focused FNX integration guide with scenario-driven examples and decision playbooks.

Scope
- Add docs: when fnx helps, when to disable, and expected tradeoffs.
- Include before/after examples with command lines and output excerpts.
- Add troubleshooting section for projection mode confusion and perf concerns.

Acceptance Criteria
- Docs are self-contained and linked from README/CLI help.
- Examples are reproducible via scripts checked into repo.
- Documentation validation scripts run in CI.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
